content.problemStatement:
You are given two strings sss and ttt. Your task is to determine whether ttt is an **anagram** of sss.

An anagram is a word formed by rearranging the letters of another word, using **all the original letters exactly the number of times it is used**.

Function DeclarationFunction NameisAnagramisAnagramisAnagram – This function checks whether one string is an anagram of another string.

Parameters
sss : A string representing the original word.
ttt : A string to be checked as an anagram of sss.
Return Value
Returns truetruetrue if ttt is an anagram of sss.
Returns falsefalsefalse otherwise.
constraints: s and t consist only of characters 'a'–'z'
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains string s, the second line contains string t. Both strings contain only lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if t is an anagram of s, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.input:
3
listen
silent
hello
world
aab
baa
sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: "silent" is a rearrangement of "listen" → YES.\nTest case 2: "world" cannot be rearranged to form "hello" → NO.\nTest case 3: "baa" uses the same letters as "aab" → YES.

solution.approach: For each test case we first compare the lengths of s and t. If they differ, they cannot be anagrams. Otherwise we count the occurrences of each of the 26 lowercase letters in s using an integer array of size 26, then decrement the same counters while scanning t. If after processing both strings all counters are zero, the two strings contain exactly the same multiset of characters and t is an anagram of s. This runs in linear time with respect to the length of the strings and uses O(1) additional memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the strings (the same for s and t). Overall O(T * N).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a fixed-size array of 26 integers is used.
